title: Service Manifest
description: Schema for services/<name>/service.yml. Each file is the single source of truth for one service family — what previously lived split across docker-compose.yml, .env.example, and bootstrapper/service-configs.yml.

virtual.description: If true, this manifest has no compose fragment. Used for logical groupings like cloud-providers (routed through LiteLLM, no container of their own). Virtual manifests MAY omit `containers` and MUST NOT have a services/<name>/compose.yml.

name.description: Folder name under services/. Must match the parent directory name. Kebab-case.

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the TUI wizard.

category.description: Wizard grouping. infra=gateway/observability; data=DB/cache/storage; llm=LLM gateway/engine; media=multimodal AI (image-gen/STT/TTS/doc/search); agents=programmable AI agents/workflows; apps=user-facing UIs.

doc_extras.description: Optional doc-generation hints. Not consumed at runtime.

extra_consumers.description: Manual escape hatch — service names to draw on the downstream tier in this service's architecture diagram, for wiring not expressible in depends_on (e.g. Kong routing).

data_flow.description: Runtime data-flow declarations consumed by the diagram resolver. Independent of depends_on (which expresses bootstrap order).

calls.description: Names of services this one calls at runtime in the request path. Init-time bootstrap calls do NOT count. Names must match either a doc-folder name under services/ or a manifest name under services/.

extra_kong_aliases.description: Additional *.localhost Kong hostnames this service exposes BEYOND its rows[].alias (e.g. a second route to a different upstream port). Unlike rows[].alias these are not wizard rows and may be multi-label (s3.minio.localhost). They land in Topology.aliases so --setup-hosts wires them; the matching Kong route is emitted by kong_config_generator. Must be unique across all manifests + rows[].alias.

containers.description: Every container name in this family's compose.yml. The manifest_validator asserts this matches compose.yml's services keys 1:1. May be empty when `virtual: true`.

var.description: Env var name that holds the image reference (interpolated as ${VAR} in compose.yml).

container.description: Which container in this manifest uses this image. Must appear in `containers`.

sources.description: Optional SOURCE-variant block. Omit for services without source variation (e.g. redis, kong).

default.description: Default source id. Must match one of options[].id.

requires.description: Env vars that MUST be non-empty when this source is selected.

profiles.description: Deployment profiles this option is offered in. OMITTED = all profiles. ["default"] = dev-only (hidden in the wizard and rejected by the validator under --profile prod); ["prod"] = prod-only.

name.description: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE env var name. Must be globally unique across all manifests.

default.description: Default value emitted into .env.example. Scalar only.

auto_managed.description: If true, the variable is computed at runtime (by bootstrapper/services/service_config.py based on the active source's runtime_sc entry) and is NOT emitted into .env.example with a default. Still documented.

secret.description: If true, treated as sensitive (.env.example default is empty; never logged in plain text).

bootstrapper_only.description: When true, this var is consumed only by the host-side bootstrapper; .env.example generator skips it.

required.description: Other service-manifest names this one CANNOT run without. Logical dependency — compose-level depends_on still lives in compose.yml.

name.description: Env var this manifest produces and other services read.

consumers.description: Service-manifest names that consume the exported value.

exports.description: Documents the cross-service env-var contract. Validator asserts each exported name appears in this manifest's env declarations (or is produced by some runtime_sc.<key>.<source>.environment block).

runtime_sc.description: Slice of the bootstrapper's legacy service-configs.yml `source_configurable:` block, owned by this manifest. The sc_synthesizer concatenates these blocks across manifests to produce the runtime config dict the bootstrapper consumes.

failure_mode.description: Single-sentence description of what happens when this adaptive dep is disabled or unreachable. Surfaced in the Dependencies & Integrations section's Failure mode column.

runtime_adaptive.description: Per-container adaptive-service descriptors. Keyed by container name in containers[].

runtime_deps.description: Slice of the legacy service-configs.yml `service_dependencies:` block.

runtime_dependency_tiers.description: Globals-only. Stack-wide tier ordering (data_tier, init_tier, core_services, app_tier).

rows.description: Box rows this manifest renders. Most manifests have one; supabase has seven (one per HTTP component). Replaces the legacy _SERVICES/_HOST_ALIAS/DISPLAY_NAME_OVERRIDES/SERVICE_DESCRIPTIONS/LOCALHOST_ENDPOINT_VARS constants.

display_name.description: Human label shown in the box and wizard prompts.

source_var.description: The *_SOURCE env var driving this row's source pick.

port_var.description: The env var holding this row's primary display port. Omit for virtual rows.

alias.description: *.localhost hostname Kong virtual-host-routes. Must be unique across all manifests.

description.description: One-line label used as wizard prompt subtitle.

localhost_endpoint_var.description: Env var holding the URL when source is a localhost variant.

localhost_port_var.description: Env var holding the user-overridable host port for the localhost source variant. Empty for services without an overridable localhost port.

secondary_number.description: Inline secondary numeric input on the row's source prompt. Used by Ray (worker count) and Prometheus (retention days). The Textual wizard mounts a SecondaryNumberInput widget; the value persists to .env on confirm.

env_var.description: The env var to persist the numeric value to.

label.description: Short label shown next to the input (e.g. 'Worker count', 'Retention (days)').

default.description: Default value as a string (the wizard converts to int).

visible_when_source.description: Source values that show the input; empty/omitted means always visible.
